label: AI Capabilities Taxonomy
description: What AI can do, organized by cognitive function type at increasing specificity for matching against business problems. INCLUDES: any AI capability deliverable today by a technical consultancy — custom builds, integrations, configurations, model customization, and deployment of open-source models. EXCLUDES: pure research, AGI, robotics/hardware, training frontier models from scratch. ROUTING RULE: route by the cognitive function the AI performs on the input — what verb describes the AI's core operation?

children.label: Generation
children.description: Creating new content that did not previously exist, across any modality. INCLUDES: text generation, code generation, image/visual creation, audio/speech synthesis, video/animation creation, synthetic data generation. EXCLUDES: condensing existing content (that's Extraction), finding existing content (that's Search), labeling existing content (that's Classification). ROUTING RULE: the AI's primary output is NEW content produced from a prompt, seed, or specification.

children.label: Text Generation
children.description: Generating natural language text from prompts or specifications. INCLUDES: copywriting, article drafting, email composition, report generation, creative writing, translation between languages, paraphrasing, dialogue generation. EXCLUDES: generating code (Code Generation), generating structured data (Synthetic Data), summarizing existing text (Extraction). ROUTING RULE: the primary output is natural language prose or structured text for human consumption.

children.label: Code Generation
children.description: Generating, completing, or transforming source code. INCLUDES: code completion, function/module generation from specs, code refactoring, bug fixing, test generation, code translation between languages, SQL generation from natural language. EXCLUDES: multi-step autonomous coding workflows with tool use (that's Agents), code review as classification (that's Classification). ROUTING RULE: the primary output is executable source code or queries.

children.label: Image & Visual Generation
children.description: Creating or modifying images and visual content. INCLUDES: text-to-image generation (diffusion models, GANs), image editing/inpainting, style transfer, design asset generation, diagram/chart generation, avatar creation, upscaling/super-resolution, synthetic image generation for training data. EXCLUDES: video generation (Video Generation), recognizing/classifying images (Classification), generating tabular/structured synthetic data (Synthetic Data Generation). ROUTING RULE: the primary output is a static visual artifact — including when generated for machine consumption (e.g., training data augmentation with synthetic images).

children.label: Audio & Speech Generation
children.description: Synthesizing audio content from text or other inputs. INCLUDES: text-to-speech (TTS), voice cloning, music generation, sound effect generation, audio style transfer, podcast generation, synthetic audio generation for training data. EXCLUDES: transcribing audio to text (that's Extraction), classifying audio (that's Classification), generating tabular/structured synthetic data (Synthetic Data Generation). ROUTING RULE: the primary output is an audio waveform or file — including when generated for machine consumption (e.g., training data augmentation with synthetic speech).

children.label: Video & Animation Generation
children.description: Creating or editing video and animated content. INCLUDES: text-to-video, video editing/compositing, animation generation, avatar animation, lip-sync generation, video style transfer, synthetic video generation for training data. EXCLUDES: static image generation (Image Generation), video classification/analysis (Classification), generating tabular/structured synthetic data (Synthetic Data Generation). ROUTING RULE: the primary output is a temporal visual sequence (video or animation) — including when generated for machine consumption (e.g., training data augmentation with synthetic video).

children.label: Synthetic Data Generation
children.description: Creating artificial TABULAR or STRUCTURED datasets for training, testing, or simulation. INCLUDES: tabular data augmentation, structured training data synthesis (CSV, JSON, database records), simulation environments, test fixture generation, privacy-preserving synthetic tabular datasets, adversarial example generation for structured inputs. EXCLUDES: generating synthetic images/audio/video for training (those route to their modality-specific Generation siblings), generating human-readable content (Text Generation), real data transformation (Extraction). ROUTING RULE: the primary output is STRUCTURED/TABULAR data intended for machine consumption or testing. Synthetic media (images, audio, video) routes to its modality-specific Generation sibling, even when used for training.

children.label: Extraction & Structuring
children.description: Comprehending unstructured inputs and pulling structured information out of them. The input is richer/messier than the output. INCLUDES: document parsing, OCR, speech-to-text transcription, entity extraction, summarization, knowledge extraction, data transformation from unstructured to structured. EXCLUDES: assigning a single label to an input (that's Classification), creating new content beyond what the source contains (that's Generation), finding documents in a corpus (that's Search). ROUTING RULE: the AI reads unstructured input and outputs structured, condensed, or reorganized information derived FROM the input.

children.label: Media Parsing & Transcription
children.description: Converting visual, audio, or video media into machine-readable text or structured data. INCLUDES: OCR (optical character recognition), form field extraction, invoice/receipt parsing, table extraction from images/PDFs, layout analysis, handwriting recognition, speech-to-text transcription, podcast/meeting transcription, video-to-text transcription, subtitle generation. EXCLUDES: extracting entities from already-digital text (Entity Extraction), summarizing content (Summarization), classifying audio (that's Classification). ROUTING RULE: the input is a non-text media format (visual document, audio, video), and the output is machine-readable text or structured data. This is the 'digitization' step — converting media to text before downstream extraction.

children.label: Entity & Attribute Extraction
children.description: Identifying and extracting specific named entities, attributes, or key-value pairs from text. INCLUDES: named entity recognition (NER — people, organizations, dates, amounts, locations), key-value extraction from contracts/forms, metadata extraction, slot filling in dialogue. EXCLUDES: extracting relationships between entities (Knowledge Extraction), assigning a category label to the whole document (Classification). ROUTING RULE: the output is a list of discrete entities or attribute-value pairs found within the text.

children.label: Summarization & Condensation
children.description: Reducing long content to its essential information. INCLUDES: abstractive summarization, extractive summarization, meeting notes generation, document TLDRs, multi-document summarization, bullet-point extraction, key takeaway identification. EXCLUDES: generating wholly new content on a topic (that's Generation), extracting structured entities (Entity Extraction). ROUTING RULE: the output is a shorter, denser version of the input that preserves its core meaning.

children.label: Knowledge & Relationship Extraction
children.description: Extracting relationships, facts, and structured knowledge from text or data. INCLUDES: knowledge graph construction, relationship extraction (entity-to-entity), causal chain extraction, fact triple generation (subject-predicate-object), ontology population, event extraction. EXCLUDES: isolated entity extraction without relationships (Entity Extraction), full document summarization (Summarization). ROUTING RULE: the output captures structured RELATIONSHIPS between concepts, not just isolated entities.

children.label: Data Transformation & Structuring
children.description: Converting data from one format or schema to another, imposing structure on semi-structured inputs. INCLUDES: unstructured-to-structured conversion, schema mapping, format normalization, log parsing, data cleaning/standardization, ETL with AI-assisted mapping. EXCLUDES: visual document digitization (Document Parsing), extracting specific entities (Entity Extraction). ROUTING RULE: the input already has SOME structure but needs reshaping, cleaning, or mapping to a target schema.

children.label: Classification & Detection
children.description: Assigning labels, categories, or binary judgments to inputs. The output is a label or score, not structured data or new content. INCLUDES: text classification, image/video recognition, structured data classification, anomaly detection, fraud detection, audio classification, content moderation. EXCLUDES: extracting structured data from inputs (Extraction), finding similar items in a corpus (Search), estimating future numeric values (Prediction). ROUTING RULE: the AI assigns a discrete label, category, or detection judgment to a given input.

children.label: Text Classification
children.description: Assigning categorical labels to text inputs. INCLUDES: sentiment analysis, intent detection, topic classification, spam detection, toxicity/content moderation, language detection, urgency/priority scoring. EXCLUDES: classifying images or audio (other Classification children), extracting entities from text (Extraction). ROUTING RULE: the input is text, the output is one or more categorical labels.

children.label: Image & Video Classification
children.description: Assigning labels or detecting objects/patterns in visual inputs. INCLUDES: object recognition, scene classification, facial recognition, object detection (bounding boxes), image segmentation, visual quality inspection, medical imaging classification, video scene detection. EXCLUDES: generating images (Generation), extracting text from images via OCR (Extraction). ROUTING RULE: the input is an image or video, the output is labels, bounding boxes, or segmentation masks.

children.label: Structured Data Classification
children.description: Assigning categorical labels to rows of tabular or structured feature data. INCLUDES: customer segmentation, lead scoring (as classification), credit approval (binary), product categorization from attributes, medical diagnosis from structured patient data, any classification where the input is a feature vector rather than unstructured content. EXCLUDES: classifying text documents (Text Classification), classifying images/video (Image & Video Classification), detecting anomalies/outliers (Anomaly Detection). ROUTING RULE: the input is STRUCTURED/TABULAR data (feature vectors, database rows, form fields), and the output is one or more categorical labels. This is the classic ML classification task.

children.label: Anomaly & Fraud Detection
children.description: Identifying outliers, fraudulent activity, or abnormal patterns in data. INCLUDES: fraud scoring, intrusion detection, manufacturing defect detection, outlier detection in time series, behavioral anomaly detection, network anomaly detection. EXCLUDES: predicting future fraud likelihood (Prediction), classifying known categories (Text/Visual Classification). ROUTING RULE: the AI identifies inputs or patterns that deviate significantly from expected/normal behavior — the label is 'anomalous vs normal', not a predefined category.

children.label: Audio & Speech Classification
children.description: Assigning labels or detecting patterns in audio inputs. INCLUDES: speaker identification/verification, emotion detection from speech, sound event detection, music genre classification, voice activity detection, accent/language identification from speech. EXCLUDES: converting speech to text (that's Extraction — transcription), generating audio (Generation). ROUTING RULE: the input is audio, the output is categorical labels about the audio's properties.

children.label: Prediction & Forecasting
children.description: Estimating future states, values, or outcomes from historical data. The output is a numeric estimate or probability about the FUTURE. INCLUDES: time series forecasting, propensity modeling, risk scoring, regression, survival analysis. EXCLUDES: classifying current inputs (Classification), detecting current anomalies (Classification), optimizing decisions (Optimization). ROUTING RULE: the AI estimates what WILL happen (or how likely something is to happen) based on historical patterns.

children.label: Time Series Forecasting
children.description: Predicting future values in a time-ordered sequence. INCLUDES: demand forecasting, revenue/sales prediction, resource utilization forecasting, stock/inventory predictions, energy consumption forecasting, traffic prediction, seasonal trend analysis. EXCLUDES: detecting anomalies in time series (that's Classification), predicting individual behavior (Propensity Modeling). ROUTING RULE: the input is historical time-ordered data, the output is projected future values along the same time axis.

children.label: Propensity & Behavioral Modeling
children.description: Predicting individual entity behavior or likelihood of specific actions. INCLUDES: churn prediction, conversion probability, customer lifetime value (CLV), purchase propensity, employee attrition likelihood, lead scoring, next-best-action prediction. EXCLUDES: aggregate time series forecasting (Time Series), risk scoring for compliance/financial purposes (Risk Scoring). ROUTING RULE: the input is features about a specific entity (customer, user, employee), the output is a probability or score for a specific future behavior.

children.label: Risk Scoring & Assessment
children.description: Estimating the probability or severity of adverse outcomes. INCLUDES: credit risk scoring, insurance risk assessment, compliance risk prediction, project risk estimation, health risk stratification, cybersecurity threat scoring. EXCLUDES: detecting fraud that is currently happening (that's Classification/Detection), behavioral propensity for non-risk actions (Propensity Modeling). ROUTING RULE: the output is a risk score or probability specifically about adverse/negative outcomes, typically for underwriting, compliance, or safety decisions.

children.label: Regression & Quantitative Estimation
children.description: Estimating continuous numeric values that don't fit neatly into time series, propensity, or risk categories. INCLUDES: pricing estimation, property valuation, yield prediction, cost estimation, age/quantity estimation, scoring models for non-behavioral attributes. EXCLUDES: forecasting along a time axis (Time Series), predicting binary behavioral outcomes (Propensity), risk-specific scoring (Risk Scoring). ROUTING RULE: the output is a continuous numeric estimate about a current or future quantity, where the question is 'how much?' rather than 'how likely?' or 'what will happen over time?'

children.label: Search & Retrieval
children.description: Finding relevant items from a corpus or catalog based on a query or context. The output is a ranked set of existing items, not new content or labels. INCLUDES: semantic search, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), recommendation systems, knowledge base Q&A, multimodal retrieval. EXCLUDES: generating new content (Generation), classifying a given input (Classification), extracting information from a single document (Extraction). ROUTING RULE: the AI selects or ranks existing items from a SET based on relevance to a query, user context, or preference signal.

children.label: Semantic Search
children.description: Finding relevant documents or passages using meaning-based similarity rather than keyword matching. INCLUDES: embedding-based document retrieval, passage retrieval, hybrid search (keyword + semantic), vector similarity search, cross-lingual search. EXCLUDES: search combined with answer generation (RAG), personalized recommendations (Recommendation). ROUTING RULE: the input is a query, the output is a ranked list of existing documents/passages, and relevance is determined by semantic similarity.

children.label: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG)
children.description: Combining document retrieval with answer generation — search first, then synthesize a response grounded in retrieved sources. INCLUDES: single-turn question-answering over documents, enterprise knowledge Q&A, citation-backed responses, multi-source synthesis, grounded answer generation. EXCLUDES: pure search without generation (Semantic Search), pure generation without retrieval grounding (Generation), multi-turn conversational interfaces using RAG (those are Conversational Agents under Agents that leverage RAG as a sub-capability). ROUTING RULE: the system BOTH retrieves from a corpus AND generates a synthesized answer in a SINGLE interaction. Multi-turn chatbots that use RAG route to Agents → Conversational Agents; RAG is the retrieval+generation TECHNIQUE, not the end-user application.

children.label: Recommendation Systems
children.description: Suggesting relevant items to users based on preferences, behavior, or context — proactive retrieval without an explicit query. INCLUDES: collaborative filtering, content-based recommendation, hybrid recommenders, next-item suggestion, personalized feeds, similar-item recommendation. EXCLUDES: search driven by an explicit user query (Semantic Search), predicting whether a user will take an action (that's Prediction). ROUTING RULE: the system proactively surfaces items a user is likely to find relevant, without the user formulating a specific query.

children.label: Multimodal & Cross-Modal Retrieval
children.description: Finding items across or between modalities — using one modality to search another. INCLUDES: image search by text description, reverse image search, audio-to-text search, video moment retrieval, cross-modal embedding search. EXCLUDES: single-modality text search (Semantic Search), classifying images (Classification). ROUTING RULE: the query and the results are in DIFFERENT modalities, or the search spans multiple modality types simultaneously.

children.label: Optimization & Planning
children.description: Finding the best solution, allocation, or sequence under constraints. The output is a decision, plan, or configuration that maximizes/minimizes an objective. INCLUDES: resource allocation, scheduling, routing, pricing optimization, experiment design, constraint satisfaction. EXCLUDES: predicting what will happen (Prediction), recommending items to users (Search), generating content (Generation). ROUTING RULE: the AI solves for the BEST choice among alternatives given explicit constraints and an objective function.

children.label: Resource Allocation & Scheduling
children.description: Optimally assigning limited resources to tasks, time slots, or entities. INCLUDES: staff scheduling, capacity planning, task assignment, workforce optimization, meeting scheduling, production scheduling, budget allocation. EXCLUDES: physical route optimization (Routing), pricing decisions (Pricing). ROUTING RULE: the decision is about WHO or WHAT gets assigned to WHERE or WHEN, given capacity constraints.

children.label: Route & Logistics Optimization
children.description: Finding optimal paths, routes, or flows through networks. INCLUDES: vehicle routing, delivery optimization, supply chain network design, warehouse layout, fleet management, last-mile delivery optimization. EXCLUDES: allocating resources to time slots (Scheduling), optimizing prices (Pricing). ROUTING RULE: the decision involves PHYSICAL or NETWORK paths — moving things through space or graph structures.

children.label: Pricing & Revenue Optimization
children.description: Setting optimal prices, bids, or financial parameters to maximize revenue or margin. INCLUDES: dynamic pricing, yield management, auction bidding strategy, markdown optimization, subscription tier optimization, promotional pricing. EXCLUDES: estimating what a price SHOULD be (that's Prediction/Regression), allocating budgets across channels (Resource Allocation). ROUTING RULE: the decision is about WHAT TO CHARGE or WHAT TO BID to maximize a financial objective.

children.label: Experiment Design & Optimization
children.description: Designing and optimizing experiments, tests, or parameter searches. INCLUDES: A/B test design, Bayesian optimization, hyperparameter tuning, multi-armed bandits, automated experimentation platforms, design of experiments (DOE). EXCLUDES: running the experiment itself, analyzing results as classification/prediction. ROUTING RULE: the decision is about WHAT TO TRY NEXT to efficiently explore a parameter space or hypothesis set.

children.label: Constraint Satisfaction & Configuration
children.description: Finding valid configurations that satisfy a set of hard and soft constraints. INCLUDES: product configuration, timetabling, matching/assignment problems (job-candidate matching), portfolio optimization, layout optimization. EXCLUDES: time-based scheduling (Scheduling), spatial routing (Routing). ROUTING RULE: the decision is about finding a VALID COMBINATION from a large configuration space where multiple constraints must be simultaneously satisfied.

children.label: Autonomous Agents & Orchestration
children.description: Multi-step reasoning with tool use, planning, and autonomous action. The AI doesn't just produce an output — it ACTS over multiple steps, using tools, APIs, or other AI capabilities to achieve a goal. INCLUDES: conversational agents, task automation agents, research agents, coding agents, multi-agent systems. EXCLUDES: single-step generation/classification/extraction (those are the underlying capabilities agents compose), simple API integrations without reasoning (that's software engineering, not AI). ROUTING RULE: the AI performs MULTIPLE reasoning steps with autonomous decisions about what to do next, using tools or other AI capabilities as building blocks.

children.label: Conversational Agents
children.description: AI systems that engage in multi-turn dialogue to accomplish goals. INCLUDES: customer service chatbots, virtual assistants, sales/onboarding agents, interactive tutors, voice assistants, helpdesk agents. EXCLUDES: single-turn text generation (Generation), RAG without conversation (Search). ROUTING RULE: the AI maintains dialogue state across multiple turns and uses conversation to achieve a goal — not just answering one question.

children.label: Task Automation Agents
children.description: AI systems that autonomously execute multi-step business workflows. INCLUDES: process automation (RPA + AI), document processing pipelines, approval workflows, data entry automation, email triage and response, form filling, multi-system orchestration. EXCLUDES: single-step extraction or classification (those are components), simple rule-based automation without AI reasoning. ROUTING RULE: the AI autonomously executes a WORKFLOW with multiple steps, making decisions about branching, error handling, and tool selection.

children.label: Research & Analysis Agents
children.description: AI systems that autonomously gather, synthesize, and report on information. INCLUDES: web research agents, competitive intelligence gathering, literature review automation, market analysis, due diligence automation, report generation from multiple sources. EXCLUDES: single-document summarization (Extraction), semantic search without synthesis (Search). ROUTING RULE: the AI autonomously GATHERS information from multiple sources and SYNTHESIZES findings — the value is in the autonomous multi-source research process.

children.label: Code & Development Agents
children.description: AI systems that autonomously perform software development tasks beyond single-step code generation. INCLUDES: autonomous coding assistants (Cursor, Claude Code), PR review agents, test-and-fix loops, codebase migration agents, CI/CD integration agents, refactoring agents. EXCLUDES: single-step code generation from a prompt (that's Generation). ROUTING RULE: the AI performs multiple development steps autonomously — reading code, planning changes, writing code, running tests, fixing failures — not just generating code from a spec.

children.label: Multi-Agent Systems
children.description: Orchestrated teams of specialized AI agents that collaborate on complex tasks. INCLUDES: agent pipelines (sequential handoff), agent teams (parallel collaboration), supervisor-worker patterns, debate/adversarial agent loops, specialized agent routing, swarm intelligence. EXCLUDES: single agents with tool use (other Agent children), simple API chaining without agent reasoning. ROUTING RULE: MULTIPLE distinct agents with different roles collaborate or are orchestrated together — not a single agent using multiple tools.

meta.purpose: classificatory/decisional — match business problems → AI capabilities → quote (timeline + price)
meta.axis: by-cognitive-function (increasing specificity: function → technique → solution pattern)
meta.leaf_level: Depth 2: capability techniques/methods with metadata. Depth 3 future: specific solution patterns.
